title: Pumpkin pie
subtitle: American spiced custard pie in a shortcrust shell
excerpt: Pumpkin pie is the American Thanksgiving custard pie: spiced pumpkin puree, condensed milk, and eggs poured into a shortcrust shell and baked at a moderate temperature until just set. The filling should wobble in the centre when it comes from the oven. The spice balance is cinnamon forward, with ginger, cloves, and nutmeg in support.
sourceNotes: Pumpkin pie is documented in American domestic cookery from the colonial period. An early version appears in Amelia Simmons's American Cookery (1796), the first American-authored cookbook, using stewed pumpkin with cream and eggs in a pastry shell. The spiced condensed milk version became standard in the late nineteenth century. The pie is inseparably associated with American Thanksgiving, where it appears as the expected dessert in virtually every regional tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the day before and refrigerate. The filling sets more firmly overnight.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; blind-bake the shell at 200 degrees C for 15 minutes, then bake the filled pie at 170 degrees C for 40 to 45 minutes until the edges are set and the centre wobbles slightly

content.text: The pastry shell is blind-baked before the filling goes in, which prevents the base from becoming soggy under the wet custard. Chill the pastry after lining the tin and before blind-baking: this relaxes the gluten and reduces shrinkage. The filling is done when the edges are fully set and the centre trembles like a just-set jelly when you shake the tin gently. It will firm to a sliceable set as it cools.

Rub 100 g cold diced butter into 175 g plain flour and 25 g icing sugar until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Add 1 egg yolk mixed with 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water and bring together into a smooth dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Roll out on a lightly floured surface and use to line a 23 cm deep pie dish. Trim, then chill for 15 minutes. Heat the oven to 200 degrees C fan. Line the pastry with baking paper, fill with baking beans, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry. Reduce the oven to 170 degrees C fan.

In a large bowl, whisk together 400 g pumpkin puree, 200 g condensed milk, 2 large eggs, 1.5 tsp cinnamon, half a teaspoon of ginger, and a quarter teaspoon each of ground cloves, nutmeg, and salt until smooth. Pour into the warm pastry shell. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes until the edges are set and the centre trembles slightly when shaken. Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before slicing.

items.problem: The filling cracked across the top
items.solution: The pie was overbaked or cooled too quickly. Remove when the centre still wobbles and cool gradually in the turned-off oven with the door ajar before refrigerating.

The blind-bake was not long enough, or the filling was added before the pastry had dried out. The base should feel dry and slightly chalky before the filling goes in. Egg-washing the base after removing the beans and baking for the final 5 minutes also helps.
